meta.name: Sub-3 Hour Marathon
meta.subtitle: Elite amateur territory
meta.description: Sub-3 hours (4:15/km) is a serious athletic achievement. This plan requires 90–110km peak weeks and significant racing experience.

weeks.title: Base Building — Week 1
weeks.note: Week 1: Build your aerobic base. Every run should feel comfortable — you're conditioning your body to handle more.

sessions.det: 8km very easy · 6:30–7:00/km · Shake out the legs. Slower than you think you need.
14km easy · 5:50–6:20/km · Conversational pace. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast.
45–60 min · Squats, lunges, hip thrusts, calf raises, core work · Injury prevention first.
14km easy · 5:50–6:20/km · Conversational pace. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast.
12km tempo · 4:45–5:05/km · Comfortably hard. You can speak in short sentences only.
28km long · 6:00–6:30/km · Easy, conversational effort throughout. The longest run of your week.

weeks.title: Peak Training — Week 16
weeks.note: Week 16: Peak training week. This is hard — it's meant to be. Trust the process and execute each session.

sessions.det: 11km very easy · 6:30–7:00/km · Shake out the legs. Slower than you think you need.
17km tempo · 4:45–5:05/km · Comfortably hard. You can speak in short sentences only.
45–60 min · Squats, lunges, hip thrusts, calf raises, core work · Injury prevention first.
13km total · 400m reps at 5K race pace · Full recovery between reps. Quality over quantity.
13km easy then 4 × 100m strides · Start slow, build to 90% in the final 30m of each stride.
33km long · 6:00–6:30/km · Easy, conversational effort throughout. The longest run of your week.
